title: The trust-latency gap: why the future of UX is intentionally slower
description: This essay challenges the conventional obsession with speed by exploring how AI's compression of decision-making creates a trust deficit that demands intentional slowness. It's a counterintuitive but critical insight for designers navigating the AI era—knowing when to decelerate is becoming a core competitive advantage.

title: Hard truths about building in the AI era | Keith Rabois (Khosla Ventures)
description: Keith Rabois delivers candid, practical wisdom on how AI is fundamentally reshaping product management, hiring strategy, and customer research methodology. This is essential listening for product leaders trying to understand what's actually changing in the AI era versus hype.

title: Most products don't need tone of voice — they need a point
description: This practical essay cuts through design dogma to argue that prescriptive tone-of-voice guidelines often create bloat rather than value—what actually matters is clarity, active language, and strategic timing. It's a refreshing call for pragmatism in design systems work.

title: Designing adaptive teams
description: This systemic analysis applies Peter Senge's learning organization principles to modern product and design teams, offering a framework for building organizations that can truly adapt to change. It's valuable for design leaders rethinking how their teams learn and evolve.

title: Rethinking design critique
description: This piece reframes design critique as a collaborative knowledge-building practice rather than a top-down evaluation, offering practical approaches to make feedback sessions more inclusive and effective. It directly addresses a pain point in design culture that affects team dynamics and output quality.
